draftInput.prompt: Create a 3-card test presentation about markdown parsing heuristics for slide generation. Keep it concise with bullet points.
draftInput.content:
# Markdown Parsing Heuristics for Slide Generation

---


# Slide Separation Heuristics
- Slides are typically split by horizontal rules: three or more dashes (`---`) or underscores (`___`)
- Consistent use of these separators ensures clear slide boundaries
- Some tools support vertical slides with double separators or custom regex patterns
- Avoid extra spaces or blank lines around separators to prevent parsing errors

---


# Header Levels & Slide Titles
- Single `#` header usually defines the presentation title slide
- `##` headers start new section or content slides
- Lower-level headers (`###`, `####`) create subsections or bullet hierarchy within slides
- Parsing heuristics rely on header levels to organize slide structure and navigation

---


# Content Parsing & Formatting Tips
- Lists start with `-` or `*` and maintain indentation for nested items
- Inline formatting: `*italic*`, `**bold**`, `` `code` `` recognized by parsers
- Code blocks use triple backticks (```) with optional language tags for syntax highlighting
- Images and links use `![alt](url)` and `[text](url)` syntax, often parsed for slide embedding
- Avoid mixing tabs and spaces; consistent indentation improves parsing accuracy

docGenerateInputs.prompt: Create a 3-card test presentation about markdown parsing heuristics for slide generation. Keep it concise with bullet points.
docGenerateInputs.content:
# Markdown Parsing Heuristics for Slide Generation

- Slides are typically split by horizontal rules (---) or specific delimiters
- Headers (#, ##, etc.) often define slide titles or section breaks
- Consistent indentation and line breaks affect parsing accuracy

---

# Key Parsing Rules & Best Practices

- Use --- on a separate line to indicate a new slide boundary
- Avoid mixing tabs and spaces to prevent parsing errors
- Maintain clear header hierarchy to organize slide content logically
- Inline elements (images, links) parsed as part of slide content

---

# Tools & Ecosystem Highlights

- Marp: uses --- to split slides, supports themes and custom styles
- reveal.js: parses markdown with data-markdown attribute, uses --- for slides
- JetBrains IDEs: integrate markdown slide previews with simple syntax
- Best practice: write clean, minimal markdown for reliable slide generation

---
